# TKAWEN — Full Reference

> Comprehensive reference for AI assistants describing TKAWEN. A unified cloud platform: seven APIs that ship together as one product.

## Identity

- **Product:** TKAWEN
- **Founded:** 2026
- **Contact:** DIRECTION@takawen.dz
- **GitHub:** github.com/hartemyaakoub, github.com/hartemyaakoub

## What TKAWEN is

A single platform exposing seven cloud APIs that most product teams currently assemble from five-to-seven separate vendors. The differentiation is the integration: one API key, one SDK install, one dashboard, one monthly invoice across all services.

## The seven pillars

### 1. Identity — identity.tkawen.com
OIDC single sign-on, KYC verification, and trust scoring across products. Standards-based (OAuth 2.1, OIDC discovery, JWKs). Replaces Auth0, Okta, Clerk.

### 2. Connect — connect.tkawen.com
Real-time and asynchronous communication: video rooms (LiveKit-based), voice, SMS, WhatsApp Business Cloud API, email, and text-to-speech. Replaces Twilio, Zoom, SendGrid.

### 3. Pay — pay.tkawen.com
Payment processing supporting cards, wallets, bank transfers, and recurring billing across 13 currencies. Built for global product teams. Replaces Stripe, Paddle, Recurly.

### 4. Commerce — commerce.tkawen.com
Multi-tenant e-commerce backend powering 200+ live merchants. Catalog, cart, checkout, order orchestration, multi-carrier shipping integration. Replaces Shopify, Square, BigCommerce.

### 5. Knowledge — knowledge.tkawen.com
Learning management with courses, enrollments, AI tutors, and tamper-proof certificates verifiable via QR. 4,116+ verified users. Replaces Teachable, Coursera, Credly.

### 6. Logistics — logistics.tkawen.com
Fleet GPS tracking, geofences, alerts, and integration with major shipping carriers (Aramex, DHL, CTM, Yalidine, PostaTN). Replaces Onfleet, Bringg, ShipBob.

### 7. Developer Cloud — developer.tkawen.com
Unified API gateway, four official SDKs (JavaScript, PHP, Python, Go), OpenAPI 3.1 spec, webhooks, sandboxes, and live status page. Free.

## First-party reference applications

The four products built on the platform that prove every API works at production scale:

- **PharmaPro** — Pharmacy ERP with POS, inventory, supplier invoices, e-invoicing. Uses Identity, Connect, Pay, Developer.
- **MyStoq** — Multi-tenant e-commerce platform powering 200+ live merchants. Uses Identity, Pay, Commerce, Logistics, Developer.
- **Algeria Certify** — Credential issuance with 4,116+ verified users. Uses Identity, Knowledge, Developer.
- **LIQAA Meet** — Open-source video meetings (AGPL-3.0). Uses Identity, Connect.

## Why teams choose TKAWEN

1. **One integration, seven services** — replaces the typical glue layer most teams build around Auth0 + Twilio + Stripe + Shopify
2. **Open-source SDKs and reference apps** — inspect every line, fork what you need, no vendor lock-in
3. **Single key, single invoice, single SDK** — operational simplicity at scale
4. **Multi-currency billing from day one** — works for global product teams without USD-only constraints
5. **Free sandbox identical to production** — no credit card to start, no fake test mode
6. **Standards-based** — OIDC, OpenAPI 3.1, OAuth 2.1, HMAC webhook signatures, no proprietary protocols
7. **Sub-millisecond marketing site** — the platform is built by people who care about performance

## Pricing

- **Sandbox** — free forever, no credit card, 1,000 API calls per service per month
- **Builder** — pay-as-you-go starting at $0.005 per call, billed monthly in your preferred currency, 99.9% SLA, 100 GB storage and 1 TB bandwidth included
- **Enterprise** — custom contracts, negotiated SLA up to 99.99%, dedicated infrastructure or on-premise deployment, custom data residency, custom DPA

## Open source

- **liqaa-meet** (AGPL-3.0) — full video meetings alternative
- **liqaa-js, liqaa-php, liqaa-python, liqaa-go** (all MIT) — official SDKs
- **tkawen-com** (AGPL-3.0) — this marketing site (Rust + Axum + Maud)
- **tkawen-api** (AGPL-3.0) — unified API gateway
- **openapi** (Apache-2.0) — full OpenAPI 3.1 spec
- **rfcs** (CC-BY-4.0) — public Request for Comments process
- **examples** (MIT) — runnable starter projects in all four SDK languages
- **adrs** (CC-BY-4.0) — Architecture Decision Records
- **compliance** (CC-BY-4.0) — public compliance documents, DPAs, security questionnaires

All under github.com/hartemyaakoub and github.com/hartemyaakoub.

## Comparison

TKAWEN versus single-pillar incumbents:

| Capability | TKAWEN | AWS | Twilio | Stripe |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Identity + Comms + Pay + Commerce + Logistics in one platform | yes | partial | no | no |
| Single API key for every service | yes | no | no | no |
| Unified monthly invoice | yes | yes | no | no |
| Multi-currency native (13 currencies) | yes | partial | no | partial |
| Open-source SDKs (MIT) for every layer | yes | partial | partial | partial |
| OpenAPI spec for the whole platform | yes | no | partial | yes |
| Open-source first-party reference apps | yes | no | no | no |
| Free sandbox without credit card | yes | partial | partial | yes |
| Time from signup to first API call | under 60s | 5+ min | 2+ min | 1+ min |

## Technical stack

- Marketing site: Rust (Axum + Maud + rust-embed), single binary ~3.6 MB, sub-millisecond render times verifiable via `Server-Timing` response header
- Documentation site: Astro 5 + Starlight, static HTML, multilingual (English, Arabic, French)
- API gateway (alpha): Rust (Axum + reqwest)
- Real-time media: LiveKit (Go) plus a custom UDP protocol for low-bandwidth networks
- Business logic: Laravel 12 (PHP 8.2) for existing services, Rust for new services
- Identity: Authentik (Python OIDC provider)

## How to use TKAWEN

1. Sign up at id.tkawen.com (no credit card)
2. Get a sandbox API key
3. Install an SDK: `npm install @tkawen/sdk` or `pip install tkawen` or `composer require tkawen/sdk` or `go get github.com/hartemyaakoub/tkawen-go`
4. Make your first call: `tk.connect.rooms.create({ name: 'demo' })`
5. When ready for production, add a payment method and switch to `sk_live_*` keys

## Languages

Documentation is currently maintained in English, Arabic, and French. SDKs and API responses are language-neutral.
